description: Confidential support for suicidal thoughts. Free, 24/7. Trained counselors available. No judgment, just support.
Text HOME to 741741 for crisis support. Connect with trained crisis counselor via text. Available for all crises.
Directory of suicide prevention centers worldwide. Find local resources by country.
Call 911/999/112 for immediate danger. Police and ambulance can respond to mental health crises including suicidal threats."
Free, confidential, 24/7 helpline for substance use and mental health. Referrals to local treatment facilities.
Support for domestic violence victims. Confidential, 24/7. Help with safety planning and resources. Also text START to 88788.
Support for LGBTQ+ individuals. Mon-Fri 4-8pm EST. Trained peer counselors. Also text chat available.
Crisis intervention for LGBTQ+ youth. Trained peer counselors. 24/7 support.
Reports of child abuse or neglect. Available 24/7. All calls confidential. Can be anonymous.
National Alliance on Mental Illness helpline. Information and referrals for mental health. Mon-Fri 10am-10pm EST.
Phone and text support for teens. Staffed by trained teens. Text TEEN to 839863. Call 1-800-825-8336.
Find licensed therapists in your area. Filter by location, insurance, specialty, and approach. Can help book appointments online.
Licensed therapists available 24/7 via video, phone, or chat. Subscription-based. Works with insurance.
Therapy via video, phone, or messaging. Licensed therapists. Works with insurance and offers financial aid.
Therapy sessions for $10-80 based on income. Sliding scale. Connect with licensed therapists across US.

resource_name: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
description: Find treatment facilities, support groups, recovery resources. Comprehensive government mental health database.
Mental health screening tools, educational resources, and referrals to local services. Supported by professionals.
Government research institution. Provides evidence-based information about mental health conditions and treatments.
Find psychologists, educational resources, research. Maintains ethical standards for psychology profession.

resource_name: Anxiety and Depression Association of America
description: Resources for anxiety and depression. Tools, educational materials, support group finder, professional directory.
Advocacy, workforce development, policy information. Connects to mental health providers and resources.
Training course teaching how to recognize and respond to mental health crises. Available online and in-person.
Walk-in psychiatric emergency service. Immediate assessment and stabilization for mental health crises. Available in most cities.
Emergency psychiatric services at local hospitals. Can go to ER for psychiatric emergency. May result in involuntary hold if danger to self/others.
24/7 peer support for mental health recovery. Connect with others who have lived experience. Combines crisis support with ongoing recovery.
